cod: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cocama.
codon: noun. A handbell used for summoning monks.
compound: noun. An enclosure within which workers, prisoners, or soldiers are confined.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
condom: noun. A flexible sleeve made of latex or other impermeable material such as polyurethane or sheepskin, worn over an erect penis during
coo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Comox.
coop: noun. A basket, pen or enclosure for birds or small animals.
cop: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Coptic.
cud: noun. The portion of food which is brought back into the mouth by ruminants from their rumen, to be chewed a second time.
cum: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Cumeral.
cup: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cupeño.
doc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Northern Kam.
don: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Doura.
dump: noun. A place where waste or garbage is left; a ground or place for dumping ashes, refuse, etc.; a disposal site.
dun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dusun Deyah.
duo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dupaningan Agta.
duomo: noun. A cathedral, or a cathedral-like building, especially one in Italy.
dup: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Duano.
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
mondo: noun. A dialogue between master and student designed to obtain an intuitive truth.
mono: noun. A bicycle or motorcycle trick where the front wheel is lifted off the ground while riding.
moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
mound: noun. An artificial hill or elevation of earth; a raised bank; an embankment thrown up for defense
mud: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mednyj Aleut.
mun: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Munda languages.
muon: noun. An unstable elementary particle in the lepton family, having similar properties to the electron but with a mass 207 times greater.
pom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Pomo.
pond: noun. An inland body of standing water, either natural or man-made, that is smaller than a lake.
pood: noun. An obsolete Russian unit of mass, equal to 40 Russian funt, or about 16.38 kg (approximately 36.11 pounds).
poon: noun. Any of several East Indian trees of the genus Calophyllum, yielding a light, hard wood used for masts, spars, etc.
pound: noun. A unit of weight in various measurement systems.
pud: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Punan Aput.
pun: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Pubian.
ump: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Umpila.
undo: verb. To reverse the effects of an action.
